flashcards.back: A graph G = (V,E) consists of a set of vertices V and a set of edges E connecting pairs of vertices.

flashcards.front: Define the order of a graph
flashcards.back: The order is the number of vertices in a graph, denoted |V| or n.

flashcards.front: Define the size of a graph
flashcards.back: The size is the number of edges in a graph, denoted |E| or m.

flashcards.front: What is the degree of a vertex?
flashcards.back: The degree of a vertex is the number of edges incident to it.
The sum of degrees of all vertices in a graph equals twice the number of edges: ∑deg(v) = 2|E|.
A complete graph Kₙ is a simple graph where every pair of distinct vertices is connected by a unique edge.
A bipartite graph is one whose vertices can be partitioned into two sets such that every edge connects a vertex from one set to a vertex from the other set.
A regular graph is one where all vertices have the same degree. If the degree is k, it's called k-regular.
An isolated vertex is a vertex with degree 0 (no incident edges).
A pendant vertex is a vertex with degree 1 (connected to exactly one other vertex).

flashcards.front: Compare adjacency matrix and adjacency list
flashcards.back: Matrix: O(V²) space, O(1) edge lookup. List: O(V+E) space, O(deg(v)) edge lookup. Matrix better for dense graphs, list better for sparse graphs.
Two graphs are isomorphic if there exists a bijection between their vertex sets that preserves adjacency.
A simple graph has no loops and no multiple edges between the same pair of vertices.
A multigraph may contain multiple edges between the same pair of vertices.
A directed edge (or arc) has a specific direction from one vertex (tail) to another vertex (head).
